title: Parameter
description: Schema for a typed action or query parameter object embedded in action or query JSON files. A parameter must have a name and may optionally include data type rules describing the expected value type. Parameter invariants are not inline in this object; they live in sibling directories validated separately with logic.schema.json. For an action parameter named 'Amount' on action key 'process_order', invariant files belong at classes/{class}/actions/process_order/parameters/amount/invariants/NNN.invariant.json (parameter directory name is the normalized parameter name: lowercase, spaces to underscores). Query parameters use classes/{class}/queries/{query_key}/parameters/{normalized_name}/invariants/NNN.invariant.json. Each invariant file is a Logic object whose type must be 'assessment' or 'let'. Invariant identity keys are pinvariant children of the parameter key. If validation fails, verify 'name' is present and non-empty.

name.description: The name of the parameter (e.g., 'amount', 'user_id', 'email_address'). This is the identifier used to reference the parameter in specifications and implementations. Must be non-empty. If validation fails: ensure this is a string with at least one character.

data_type_rules.description: Optional data type rules describing the expected value type and constraints for the parameter (e.g., 'positive integer', 'non-empty string', 'valid email address'). Helps implementers understand the expected input format.

type_spec.description: Optional type specification for the parameter's data type in TLA+ notation. Provides a precise formal type for the parameter (e.g., 'Nat', 'STRING', 'Int'). Only meaningful when data_type_rules can be parsed into a data type.

nullable.description: Whether absent (NULL) is a valid value for this parameter. Defaults to false when omitted.
